subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Uses of Crops: Cash and Industrial Crops

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Nutrients from Crops
context: Learning nutrients crops provide: carbohydrates (energy), proteins (body building), fats and oils, vitamins, minerals for health

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

context: Identifying farm animals: work animals (oxen, horses), dairy animals (cows, goats), poultry (chickens, turkeys), aquatic (fish), pets

subject: Agricultural Science

context: Learning to identify farm animals by external features: body covering, number of legs, body shape, size, color patterns

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Factors of Production: Capital
context: Understanding money and assets for farming: purchasing inputs (seeds, fertilizers), farm tools, machinery, buildings, working capital

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Factors of Production: Water
context: Learning water's importance: crop irrigation, animal drinking, processing farm produce, essential for all agricultural activities

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Factors of Production: Management
context: Understanding farm management: planning, organizing, decision-making, supervising workers, record keeping, marketing produce

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Importance of Each Production Factor
context: Understanding how each factor contributes: land provides space, labor provides work, capital provides resources, water sustains life, management coordinates all

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Agricultural Science

context: Hands-on practice: clearing vegetation, tilling soil, making beds or ridges, applying manure, preparing land for planting

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: School Farm: Planting and Maintenance
context: Practical activities: seed selection, planting at correct spacing and depth, watering, weeding, observing crop growth stages

subject: Agricultural Science

subject: Basic Science

subject: Basic Science
topic: Benefits and Dangers of Space Travel

subject: Computer Studies

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Disadvantages of Propagation Methods
context: Learning drawbacks of seed propagation (dormancy, reduces consumption) and vegetative propagation (disease transfer, storage issues)

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Cultural Practices in Crop Production
context: Understanding thinning, supplying (gap filling), mulching, and other farm management practices for crop growth

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Pre-Planting Operations
context: Learning site selection, land clearing, stumping, plotting, tilling, ploughing, harrowing, ridging, and seed selection

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Nursery and Nursery Practices
context: Understanding nursery establishment, seedling care, transplanting techniques for successful crop establishment

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Planting Operations
context: Learning seed treatment methods, proper spacing, and planting techniques for different crops

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Post-Planting Operations
context: Understanding thinning, supplying, mulching, manuring, fertilizer application for crop development and growth

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Harvesting: Timing and Methods
context: Learning proper harvesting time, methods, and effects of timely versus late harvesting on crop quality

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Post-Harvest Operations
context: Understanding crop processing into useable forms, storage methods, and preservation techniques

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Animal Feeds: Meaning and Types
context: Learning about animal feed, types of feedstuffs: energy-yielding feeds and protein-yielding feeds

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Feeding Tools and Equipment
context: Understanding feeding tools like troughs, drinker sets; using feedstuffs to feed farm animals properly

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Farm Animal Diseases and Causative Organisms
context: Learning diseases caused by bacteria, fungi, viruses, protozoa, worms: mastitis, ringworm, foot-and-mouth, coccidiosis

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Transmission and Symptoms of Animal Diseases
context: Understanding modes of transmission (contact, vectors) and symptoms: fever, diarrhea, weight loss, reduced productivity

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Effects and Control of Animal Diseases
context: Learning disease effects on productivity; prevention through sanitation, medication, immunization, isolation

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Visit to Abattoir and Meat Inspection
context: Practical exposure to meat inspection, understanding disease identification and food safety in livestock production

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Fishery: Definition and Classification
context: Understanding fishery; classifying fish by habitat (freshwater, saltwater) and morphology (bony, cartilaginous)

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Types of Aquatic Organisms
context: Learning about shellfish (crab, prawn), reptiles (turtle, crocodile), mammals (hippopotamus, whale) in fisheries

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Uses of Fish and Fish Products
context: Understanding fish as food source, income generation, protein supply, and other economic benefits

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Methods of Fishing
context: Learning different fishing techniques using nets, hooks, baskets, and traps for catching fish

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Risk Factors in Fish Farming
context: Understanding water pollution from chemicals, industrial effluents, and contamination from fishing chemicals

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Types of Forests in Nigeria
context: Learning about mangrove forests (swampy), rainforests (many trees), derived forests (few trees) and their characteristics

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Forest Resources
context: Understanding forest resources: trees, wildlife, fruits, herbs, shelter and their importance to humans

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Uses of Forest Resources
context: Learning economic, environmental, and social uses of forest products like timber, fruits, medicinal herbs, wildlife

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Effects of Forest on Environment
context: Understanding how forests influence climate, prevent erosion, purify air, provide habitat for wildlife and biodiversity

subject: Agricultural Science
topic: Human Activities Affecting Forests
context: Learning about deforestation, bush burning, hunting, farming activities that deplete forest resources and wildlife

subject: Basic Science
topic: Living Things - Habitat and Adaptations
context: Introduction to different habitats (terrestrial, aquatic, arboreal) and how organisms adapt to survive in their specific environments through structural and behavioral features

subject: Basic Science
topic: Relationships Between Organisms in Habitats
context: Study of ecological relationships including predator-prey, competition, symbiosis, and how organisms interact within their shared habitats

subject: Basic Science
topic: Uniqueness of Human Beings
context: Exploration of human intelligence skills including reasoning, problem-solving, observation, measurement, and inference that distinguish humans from other living things

subject: Basic Science
topic: Growth and Development in Living Things
context: Understanding indices of growth changes and developmental stages in humans from infancy through adolescence to adulthood, including temporary and permanent changes

subject: Basic Science
topic: Chemicals - Meaning and Classification
context: Introduction to chemicals, their classification based on use (pharmaceutical, agrochemical, industrial) and hazardous nature (toxic, non-toxic)

subject: Basic Science
topic: Safety Measures in Chemical Use
context: Learning proper handling, storage, and safety precautions when using chemicals including understanding safety signs and manufacturer instructions

subject: Basic Science
topic: Work, Energy and Power
context: Understanding the concepts of work, energy, and power including the relationship between them and basic calculations using the formula Power = Work done / Time

subject: Basic Science
topic: Potential and Kinetic Energy
context: Distinguishing between potential energy (stored energy) and kinetic energy (energy in motion) with practical examples and demonstrations

subject: Basic Science
topic: Energy Transfer and Work Done
context: Exploring how energy is transferred when work is done and applying formulas to calculate work done in different scenarios

subject: Basic Science
topic: Kinetic Theory of Matter
context: Understanding the assumptions of kinetic theory and using it to explain the molecular structure and behavior of solids, liquids, and gases

subject: Basic Science
topic: Kinetic Theory - States of Matter
context: Detailed explanation of how kinetic theory accounts for the properties and differences between solids, liquids, and gases based on molecular arrangement and movement

subject: Basic Science
topic: Boiling and Evaporation
context: Distinguishing between boiling and evaporation processes using kinetic theory and understanding the molecular changes during phase transitions

subject: Basic Science
topic: Factors Affecting Evaporation
context: Investigating factors that influence the rate of evaporation including temperature, surface area, humidity, and air movement

subject: Basic Science
topic: Heat Flow and Transfer
context: Understanding that heat flows from hot bodies to cold bodies when in contact and introduction to the three methods of heat transfer

subject: Basic Science
topic: Heat Conduction
context: Exploring heat transfer through conduction in solids, identifying good and poor conductors, and practical applications of conduction

subject: Basic Science
topic: Heat Convection
context: Understanding heat transfer through convection in fluids (liquids and gases), observing convection currents, and applications in daily life

subject: Basic Science
topic: Heat Radiation
context: Learning about heat transfer through radiation without a medium, understanding factors affecting radiation, and practical applications

subject: Basic Science
topic: Crude Oil - Formation and Meaning
context: Introduction to crude oil and petrochemicals, their formation, composition, and significance as natural resources

subject: Basic Science
topic: Refining of Crude Oil
context: Understanding the fractional distillation process used to refine crude oil into various useful products like petrol, kerosene, and diesel

subject: Basic Science
topic: Uses and Importance of Crude Oil
context: Exploring the various uses of petroleum products and the economic importance of crude oil to Nigeria and global development

subject: Basic Science
topic: Biodiversity in Different Habitats
context: Exploring the variety of living organisms found in different habitats and the importance of biodiversity for ecosystem balance

subject: Basic Science
topic: Conservation of Habitats
context: Understanding threats to habitats including pollution and deforestation, and methods of habitat conservation and environmental protection

subject: Basic Science
topic: Human Impact on Environment
context: Examining how human activities affect the environment both positively and negatively, including pollution, urbanization, and conservation efforts

subject: Basic Science
topic: Measurement and Scientific Skills
context: Practical application of measurement skills including length, mass, time, and temperature using appropriate instruments and units

subject: Basic Science
topic: Chemical Safety in the Laboratory
context: Hands-on practice with laboratory safety rules, proper labeling and storage of chemicals, and emergency procedures

subject: Basic Science
topic: Energy Conservation
context: Understanding the principle of conservation of energy, energy efficiency, and ways to reduce energy waste in daily activities

subject: Basic Science
topic: Sources of Energy
context: Identifying renewable and non-renewable energy sources including solar, wind, fossil fuels, and their advantages and disadvantages

subject: Basic Science
topic: Heat and Temperature
context: Distinguishing between heat and temperature, understanding thermometers and temperature scales (Celsius, Fahrenheit), and practical measurements

subject: Basic Science
topic: Applications of Heat Transfer
context: Real-world applications of conduction, convection, and radiation in cooking, heating systems, insulation, and industrial processes

subject: Basic Science
topic: Petrochemicals and Modern Life
context: Exploring the wide range of petrochemical products (plastics, cosmetics, medicines) and their impact on modern society and environment

subject: Basic Technology
topic: First Aid - Meaning and Materials
context: Introduction to first aid, understanding what it means to provide immediate care before professional medical help arrives, and identifying contents of a first aid box including bandages, cotton wool, antiseptics, plasters, scissors, and thermometer

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Application of Simple First Aid
context: Practical lessons on applying basic first aid measures for minor injuries like cuts, bruises, burns, and sprains, learning proper techniques for cleaning wounds and applying bandages

subject: Basic Technology
topic: ABC of First Aid - Airways, Bleeding, Circulation
context: Learning the fundamental ABC approach to first aid: ensuring clear Airways for breathing, controlling Bleeding and dealing with Breaks (fractures), and checking Circulation through pulse monitoring

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Application of ABC First Aid to Accident Victims
context: Practical application of ABC first aid principles to help victims of common workshop accidents and road accidents, understanding priority actions and safety procedures when assisting injured persons

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Rescue Operations - Meaning and Aspects
context: Understanding what rescue operations entail, learning about different aspects including securing the accident environment, protecting vehicles involved, and ensuring safety of victims before providing assistance

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Steps in Rescue Operations
context: Learning the sequential steps involved in conducting effective rescue operations including scene assessment, calling for help, securing the area with caution signs, and safely extracting victims

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Materials and Their Common Uses - Wood
context: Exploring common uses of wood materials in everyday life including furniture making, building construction, tool handles, and decorative items, understanding wood as a versatile natural material

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Materials and Their Uses - Metals and Alloys
context: Understanding specific uses of ferrous metals (containing iron) and non-ferrous metals (brass, bronze, aluminum, copper) and their alloys in household utensils, vehicles, ships, decorations, and ammunition

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Materials and Their Uses - Ceramics, Glass, Rubber
context: Exploring uses of ceramics in tiles and household utensils, glass in windows and containers, and rubber in tires, tubes, and footwear, understanding their unique properties and applications

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Materials and Their Uses - Plastics
context: Learning about various plastic products like buckets, bottles, containers, and their widespread use in modern life, understanding advantages of plastics including durability, light weight, and water resistance

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Geometric Construction - Parts of a Circle
context: Understanding and identifying parts of a circle including diameter, radius, chord, sector, quadrant, circumference, and arc, learning to define and draw these components accurately

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Circle Drawing and Division
context: Using drawing instruments (set squares, tee square, compasses) to draw circles and divide them into 4, 8, and 12 equal parts, developing precision and accuracy in technical drawing

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Construction of Tangents and Normals to Circles
context: Learning to construct tangents (lines touching circle at one point) and normals (perpendicular to tangent) to a circle: tangent at a point on the circle, from a point outside, and to two equal circles

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Triangles - Types and Construction
context: Defining, identifying, and constructing various types of triangles including right-angled, equilateral, isosceles, and scalene triangles using appropriate drawing instruments and techniques

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Inscribed and Circumscribed Circles to Triangles
context: Learning to construct circles inscribed inside triangles (touching all three sides) and circles circumscribed around triangles (passing through all three vertices) using compass and ruler

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Polygons - Definition and Construction
context: Understanding regular polygons (equal sides and angles) and irregular polygons, learning to construct pentagon (5 sides), hexagon (6 sides), heptagon (7 sides), and octagon (8 sides) using general and specific methods

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Plane Figures - Construction and Equal Areas
context: Identifying and constructing regular plane figures, learning to construct triangles and rectangles of equal areas, and squares and rectangles of equal areas through geometric methods

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Areas of Regular Plane Figures
context: Learning to calculate and determine the areas of regular plane figures including triangles, rectangles, and squares using mathematical formulas and geometric principles

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Enlargement and Reduction of Plane Figures
context: Learning techniques to enlarge and reduce plane figures (triangles, rectangles, squares) in given ratios using two methods: length of sides method and radial line method for scaling drawings

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Woodwork Machines - Types and Uses
context: Identifying various woodwork machines including portable power tools (belt sander, hand drill, fret saw) and stationary machines (circular saw, band saw, wood lathe, surface planer, thickness planer, sanders)

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Woodwork Machine Operations
context: Learning to carry out simple operations like cutting and boring wood using woodwork machines safely, understanding functions of different machines and practicing basic woodworking techniques under supervision

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Care and Maintenance of Woodwork Machines
context: Understanding proper care and maintenance procedures for woodwork machines including cleaning, lubrication, blade sharpening, and safety checks to ensure longevity and safe operation

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Metalwork Machines - Types and Uses
context: Identifying various metalwork machines including centre lathe, power hacksaw, pedestal drilling machine, milling machine, shaping machine, and grinding machine, understanding their specific functions and applications

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Metalwork Machine Operations and Maintenance
context: Learning to perform simple operations (cutting and drilling metals) using metalwork machines safely, and understanding care and maintenance procedures for these machines in the workshop

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Belt and Chain Drives - Principles and Applications
context: Understanding belt drives (using belts to transfer power) and chain drives (using chains like in bicycles and motorcycles), learning their working principles, applications in machines like fans, grinders, and sewing machines

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Advantages and Disadvantages of Belt and Chain Drives
context: Analyzing the advantages (smooth operation, shock absorption) and disadvantages (slippage in belts, noise in chains, maintenance needs) of belt and chain drive systems in various machines

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Hydraulic and Pneumatic Machines - Components
context: Identifying hydraulic machines (using liquid pressure like hydraulic jacks, force pumps) and pneumatic machines (using air pressure like air compressors, spray guns), learning about their main components

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Working Principles of Hydraulic and Pneumatic Devices
context: Understanding how hydraulic devices work using Pascal's principle (pressure transmitted through liquids) and pneumatic devices work using compressed air, exploring their uses in industry and everyday applications

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Gears - Types, Uses and Gear Ratios
context: Learning about different types of gears (internal, external, bevel gears), their uses in power transmission, changing direction, and selecting speed, calculating gear ratios and understanding their relationship to speed of rotation

subject: Basic Technology
topic: Functions of Lubricants and Gear Construction
context: Understanding why lubricants are essential in gears and moving parts (reducing friction, preventing wear, heat dissipation), and practical construction of simple gears using cardboard or wood

subject: Civic Education
topic: National Values - Integrity: Meaning and Attributes
context: Understanding integrity as adherence to moral and ethical principles, learning attributes including probity (honesty in dealings), honesty, truthfulness, fair-play, contentment, and non-compromise of principles

subject: Civic Education
topic: Integrity - People of Integrity and Their Impact
context: Identifying examples of people of integrity in society and the community, understanding the need for integrity in achieving community development, peaceful coexistence, and serving as role models for young people

subject: Civic Education
topic: National Values - Contentment: Meaning and Attributes
context: Understanding contentment as being satisfied with what one has, learning attributes including satisfaction, lack of envy, rejection of greed and corruption, humility, and appreciating one's current circumstances

subject: Civic Education
topic: Contentment - Effects of Lack of Contentment
context: Examining the negative effects of lack of contentment on society including corruption, greed, envy, theft, prostitution, robbery, cheating, and other social vices, learning proverbs and adages that promote contentment

subject: Civic Education
topic: National Values - Discipline: Meaning and Attributes
context: Understanding discipline as controlled behavior and obedience to rules, learning attributes including self-control, moderation, modesty, respect for legitimate authority, respect for rules and regulations, perseverance, and dedication

subject: Civic Education
topic: Discipline - Benefits and Consequences of Indiscipline
context: Understanding the benefits of disciplined behavior for individuals and society, examining the consequences of indiscipline including social disorder, academic failure, criminal behavior, and loss of opportunities

subject: Civic Education
topic: National Values - Courage: Meaning and Types
context: Understanding courage as the ability to face danger or difficulty despite fear, identifying types of courage including physical courage (facing physical danger), moral courage (standing for what is right), and religious courage (standing for faith)

subject: Civic Education
topic: Courage - Attributes of Courageous Individuals
context: Learning attributes of courageous individuals and societies including bravery, determination, standing up for truth and justice, facing fears, defending the weak, and maintaining principles despite opposition or pressure

subject: Civic Education
topic: Nigeria as a Federation - Meaning and Need
context: Understanding federation as a system of government where power is divided between central and regional governments, learning the needs for federation including national integration, unity in diversity, and controlling activities of states

subject: Civic Education
topic: Characteristics of a Federation
context: Learning key characteristics of federal systems including sharing of powers between central and state governments, states having constitutionally defined control over their affairs, constitutional framework, and balance of power

subject: Civic Education
topic: Three Tiers of Government in Nigeria
context: Understanding the three levels of government in Nigeria: Federal Government (national level), State Government (regional level), and Local Government (grassroots level), and their constitutional roles and responsibilities

subject: Civic Education
topic: Relationship Among Federal, State and Local Governments
context: Understanding the nature of relationships among the three tiers including performing functions according to constitutional provisions, power to legislate, executive functions, and providing forms of governance at different levels

subject: Civic Education
topic: Differences Among Three Tiers of Government
context: Learning differences in functions, legislative powers, executive powers between Federal, State and Local Governments, understanding exclusive powers (only federal), concurrent powers (shared), and residual powers (state-specific)

subject: Civic Education
topic: The Rule of Law - Meaning and Benefits
context: Understanding law as rules governing society and rule of law as principle that everyone is subject to law, learning benefits of rule of law over arbitrary rule including justice, fairness, protection of rights, and orderly society

subject: Civic Education
topic: Punishment for Breaking the Law
context: Learning about offences under the law and their punishments, understanding that legal consequences apply to all lawbreakers regardless of status, exploring common offences like theft, assault, fraud, and their legal penalties

subject: Civic Education
topic: Equality Before the Law
context: Understanding the principle 'the law is no respecter of persons' meaning all citizens are equal before the law regardless of wealth, position, religion, or ethnicity, learning about fair trial and equal justice

subject: Civic Education
topic: Protection of Human Rights - Ways and Methods
context: Learning ways of protecting human rights including representing affected persons in court, protest marches, media coverage, hunger strikes, and drawing public attention to human rights violations

subject: Civic Education
topic: Groups That Protect Human Rights
context: Identifying groups that assist in protecting citizens' human rights including Civil Society Organizations (CLO, CDHR, CRP), trade unions, student unions, ethnic associations, and Legal Aid Council, understanding their roles

subject: Civic Education
topic: Consumer Rights - Universal Rights
context: Learning the eight universal consumer rights including right to satisfaction of basic needs, safety, information, choice, redress, consumer education, representation/be heard, and healthy environment

subject: Civic Education
topic: Consumer Responsibilities
context: Understanding consumer responsibilities including being aware of rights, demanding and keeping proofs of transactions (receipts, invoices, warranties), thinking independently, speaking out against injustice, and respecting the environment

subject: Civic Education
topic: Democracy - Meaning and Features
context: Understanding democracy as government of the people, by the people, for the people, learning key features including popular participation, rule of law, free and fair elections, separation of powers, and protection of rights

subject: Civic Education
topic: Democratic Institutions and Their Functions
context: Identifying democratic institutions in Nigeria including INEC (conducting elections), political parties (organizing politics), pressure groups (influencing policy), and three arms of government (legislature, executive, judiciary) with their functions

subject: Civic Education
topic: Importance and Benefits of Democracy
context: Learning the benefits of democracy to citizens and the nation including political freedom, protection of rights, accountability of leaders, citizen participation in governance, peaceful change of government, and development

subject: Civic Education
topic: Pillars of Democracy
context: Understanding the pillars that support democracy including the people (citizens as foundation), democratic institutions, rule of law, transparent electoral process, majority rule with minority rights protection, and free press

subject: Civic Education
topic: Elections - Concepts and Types
context: Understanding election (process of choosing leaders by voting), voter (person eligible to vote), and voting (act of casting ballot), differentiating between types of elections including general, by-election, primary, local, and referendum

subject: Civic Education
topic: Qualifications for Election and Voter Responsibilities
context: Learning basic qualifications for candidates seeking election including age, citizenship, education, mental soundness, and understanding voter responsibilities including registering, voting peacefully, reporting malpractice, and accepting results

subject: Civic Education
topic: Voting Procedures and Importance of Elections
context: Learning the step-by-step procedure for voting including voter registration, collection of voter cards, accreditation, casting votes, and vote counting, understanding why elections are important for democracy and good governance

subject: Civic Education
topic: Electoral Malpractices - Meaning and Forms
context: Understanding electoral malpractice as illegal actions during elections, identifying forms including fake ballot papers, vote manipulation, multiple voting, underage voting, thuggery, intimidation, financial inducement, and ballot box snatching

subject: Civic Education
topic: Causes and Consequences of Electoral Malpractices
context: Examining causes of electoral malpractices like greed, desperation for power, weak institutions, poverty, and voter apathy, understanding consequences including electoral violence, illegitimate government, loss of trust, and political instability

subject: Civic Education
topic: Preventing Electoral Malpractices
context: Learning measures to prevent electoral malpractices including voter education, strengthening INEC, enforcing electoral laws, ensuring security at polling units, transparent processes, citizen vigilance, and reporting suspicious activities

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Computer Software - Types and Examples
context: Understanding the definition of software and distinguishing between system software (operating systems) and application software (word processing, spreadsheet, graphics)

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Operating Systems
context: Learning about operating systems including DOS, Windows, Linux, and Unix, and understanding their functions such as resource allocation, monitoring, and utilities

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Units of Storage in Computer
context: Understanding computer storage units including nibble, byte, kilobyte, megabyte, and gigabyte, and learning to convert between different units

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Computer Programming Languages
context: Introduction to computer programs and programming languages such as Logo and BASIC, understanding what makes a programming language

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Writing Simple BASIC Programs
context: Practical experience in writing, running, and debugging simple BASIC programs using basic statements and commands

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Computer Ethics and Responsible Use
context: Learning responsible ways of using computers and the internet including proper maintenance, avoiding liquid damage, using dust covers, and email etiquette

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Safety Measures When Using Computers
context: Understanding safety precautions including proper sitting posture, anti-glare protectors, monitor positioning, room illumination, and maintaining dust-free environment

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Introduction to Graphic Packages
context: Learning about graphic packages such as Paint, CorelDraw, and Instant Artist, and understanding their features like toolbar, menu bar, and color palette

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Paint Environment and Tools
context: Exploring the Paint program environment, identifying paint tools and their functions, and understanding the interface components

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Drawing with Paint
context: Practical lessons on using Paint tools to draw and color simple objects such as the Nigerian flag, shapes, and basic illustrations

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Advanced Paint Techniques
context: Learning advanced features of Paint including text insertion, image editing, special effects, and creating more complex drawings

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Information Technology as a Transformational Tool
context: Understanding what ICT stands for and identifying IT gadgets like computers, telephones (GSM), satellite communication, television, and internet

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Benefits and Disadvantages of IT
context: Exploring the advantages of information technology in daily life, education, and business, as well as understanding potential disadvantages and challenges

subject: Computer Studies
topic: IT Gadgets - GSM, Fax, and Telephone
context: Learning to differentiate between GSM, fax machines, and telephones, and practical experience in making calls, sending messages, and storing information

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Using GSM Handsets
context: Hands-on practice with GSM handsets including creating and sending messages, storing contacts, saving text messages, and managing phone features

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Introduction to the Internet
context: Understanding the internet as the largest computer network, learning about email addresses, worldwide web (www), and internet browsers like Internet Explorer and Mozilla

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Creating and Using Email
context: Practical lessons on creating email accounts, writing valid email addresses, opening emails, and understanding email etiquette and communication

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Benefits and Abuses of the Internet
context: Understanding internet benefits (information exchange, e-learning, e-entertainment) and abuses (fraud, pornography) with emphasis on safe internet practices

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Internet Environment and Icons
context: Recognizing and naming icons in the internet environment, understanding their functions, and navigating web browsers effectively

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Sending and Receiving Emails
context: Practical experience in composing emails, sending and receiving messages, managing inbox, and organizing email communications

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Internet Chat and Communication
context: Learning to use internet chat features for real-time communication, understanding chat etiquette, and safe online interaction practices

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Educational Network Groups
context: Linking up with educational network groups such as SchoolNet and e-school, exploring educational websites, and accessing online learning resources

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Web Browsing and Navigation
context: Learning to navigate websites, use search engines effectively, understand website addresses (URLs), and access specific web pages

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Computer Maintenance and Care
context: Understanding proper computer maintenance including cleaning, protecting from power problems, using surge protectors, and unplugging when not in use

subject: Computer Studies
topic: File Management and Organization
context: Learning to create, save, organize, and retrieve files and folders on a computer system, understanding file naming conventions and directory structure

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Introduction to Other Graphic Software
context: Exploring other graphic packages beyond Paint such as CorelDraw and Instant Artist, comparing features and capabilities of different graphic software

subject: Computer Studies
topic: Practical Graphics Project
context: Students create a complete graphics project using learned skills, incorporating drawing, coloring, text, and design elements to produce original artwork

subject: Computer Studies
topic: IT in Modern Society
context: Exploring the role of information technology in various sectors including education, healthcare, banking, entertainment, and communication in modern Nigerian society

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Elements of Design
context: Introduction to the basic components of design including line, colour, form, space, and texture, and how to apply them in creating visual compositions

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Principles of Design
context: Understanding design principles such as harmony, variety, balance, proportion, dominance, and rhythm, and their application in artwork

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Materials, Tools and Equipment for Craft Making
context: Learning about different materials (paper, fabric, clay, wood) and tools (scissors, pliers, throwing wheel) used in various craft productions

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: The Study of Colours
context: Exploring colour theory including classification of colours (primary, secondary, tertiary), colour mixing, local colour sources, and creating a colour wheel

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Colour Application in Crafts
context: Practical application of colour mixing techniques and applying colours to various craft works using appropriate tools and materials

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Painting Techniques
context: Learning to paint objects from observation including still life, nature, landscape, and figures using tones, tints, shapes, and hues

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Production of Patterns
context: Understanding different types of patterns (simple repeat, half drop, mirror repeat, diamond repeat) and creating pattern designs

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Bead Work and Jewellery Making
context: Introduction to bead making using materials like roll paper, seeds, straw, and wood to create jewellery items such as necklaces and bangles

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: History and Origin of Music
context: Exploring the origins of music from natural sounds and rhythms, and studying early forms of music from ancient civilizations like Baroque period

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Musical Practices - Listening and Appreciation
context: Developing aural skills through listening to various popular music genres (Highlife, Fuji, Juju, Gospel, R&B) and identifying African and Western musical styles

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Singing and Voice Training
context: Learning to sing the Nigerian National Anthem, running scales, pitch identification, and voice training exercises for proper vocal control

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Design in the Theatre
context: Understanding theatre design elements including set design, costumes, lighting, make-up, sound, and properties used in theatrical productions

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Rehearsal in Theatre
context: Learning the stages and activities involved in theatrical rehearsals from script reading to blocking, polishing, and dress rehearsal

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Types of Dance
context: Exploring different dance types including pure dance, dance drama, dramatic dance, and comic dance, and understanding their unique characteristics

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Dance as a Career
context: Understanding career prospects in dance including working as a choreographer, dance educator, consultant, and opportunities in the entertainment industry

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Self Control and Character Development
context: Learning the meaning of self control, ways to exercise moderation and modesty, benefits of self control, and practicing self-discipline

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Advanced Craft Production - Paper Craft
context: Practical production of paper crafts using techniques like cutting, folding, and pasting to create decorative and functional items

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Fabric Decoration and Dyeing
context: Introduction to fabric craft including tie-dye techniques, batik, and other fabric decoration methods using natural and synthetic dyes

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Moulding and Sculpture
context: Learning to work with clay, plasticine, and paper mache to create three-dimensional moulded artworks and simple sculptures

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Introduction to Carving
context: Basic wood carving techniques using appropriate tools like chisels, and creating simple carved designs and functional items

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Weaving and Basketry
context: Learning traditional weaving techniques using materials like palm fronds, raffia, and creating woven items such as baskets and mats

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Musical Instruments and Classification
context: Identifying and classifying musical instruments into families (string, wind, percussion) and understanding their use in different music styles

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Theatre Performance and Production
context: Practical application of theatre skills through class play production including acting, character development, and stage performance

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Nigerian Traditional Dance Forms
context: Exploring various Nigerian traditional dances, their cultural significance, costumes, and movements specific to different ethnic groups

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
topic: Stage Makeup and Costume Design
context: Learning basic stage makeup techniques and costume design principles for different character types in theatrical performances

subject: Cultural and Creative Arts
